content.title: Couple Prays Together

instructions.caption: You want a relationship that lasts? Start doing THIS every single morning. 🙏🏾💯 The real flex isn't matching outfits; it's a matching prayer life. We are talking about true sacrifice today.
instructions.sound: Use a trending cinematic ambient instrumental (like slow piano or emotional strings). Set the background track volume to exactly 15% so Teddy's voice punches through clearly without losing the emotional undertone.
instructions.postingTime: Tuesday at 7:00 AM EST. You want to catch people while they are currently in their own morning routines or commuting, making the message immediately relevant.
instructions.thumbnailStrategy: Select the exact visual frame at 0:01 where the text overlay reads "LOVE IS PRAYING TOGETHER EVERY MORNING."

instructions.caption: Stop leaving the house without doing this first. 🛑 Teddy drops a massive truth bomb on Sacrificial Conversations about what it really means to operate as one. Making the bed and holding hands in prayer before walking out the door changes the entire trajectory of your day. It's a sacrifice of your time, but an investment in your soul. Are you and your partner doing this? 👇🏾 Let's talk about it in the comments!
instructions.strategyNote: Pin a comment immediately after posting asking: "What is the hardest part about building a consistent prayer routine with your spouse?" This sparks debate and shared testimonies, driving up engagement metrics.
instructions.postingTime: Sunday at 6:00 PM EST. This captures the high engagement window for faith-based content right after church and perfectly primes the audience for your live radio show on Monday morning.
instructions.thumbnailStrategy: A high-contrast frame showing Teddy looking emotional, with a bold text overlay reading: "The Ultimate Morning Routine."

instructions.caption:
This Morning Routine Will Save Your Marriage 🙏🏾 #Shorts

Description: True love requires sacrificing your morning rush to cover your family in prayer. Listen to why making the bed and holding hands before leaving the house is the ultimate game-changer for your relationship. Tune in to Sacrificial Conversations LIVE every Monday on WFTB 104.1 TabNashville! Watch the full episode here: [Insert Full Video Link]
instructions.pinnedComment: What's one thing you and your partner do every morning to stay connected? Let us know below! 👇🏾
instructions.postingTime: Monday at 12:00 PM EST.
instructions.thumbnailStrategy: A close-up frame of Teddy holding back tears with the text "God has blessed us."
instructions.audienceSettings: Is this video made for kids? Select NO. Age Restriction: Select NO.
instructions.relatedVideo: Action: Link this Short directly to the full-length interview. Why: Teddy mentions "finally getting to this point." This creates a powerful curiosity gap. Viewers will click the full video to find out what struggles they sacrificed to finally reach this season of peace.

instructions.caption: It's easy to rush out the door. It's easy to let the chaos of the morning dictate your spirit. But true sacrifice in a marriage looks like stopping the clock, making the bed together, and holding hands to cover your family in prayer. In this powerful moment from Sacrificial Conversations, Teddy opens up about the beauty of finally reaching a point where his heart is "operating outside of his chest" through daily, shared gratitude. It's not about being perfect; it's about being unified before the world tries to pull you apart. What are you sacrificing to keep your foundation strong? Catch the full conversation on our YouTube channel or listen live Mondays on WFTB 104.1 TabNashville. [Insert Link]
instructions.shareableQuote: Love is praying together every morning. We make the bed together, we hold hands, and we cover everyone.
instructions.cta: Has a morning prayer routine transformed your relationship? Share your testimony in the comments below to encourage someone else today!
instructions.postingTime: Wednesday at 6:00 AM EST for the morning inspiration slot, as Facebook demographics lean toward early-morning reading.

content.title: Avoid Road Rage - Couples Podcast
content.thumbnail: /assets/week 2 media/week 2 new media/AVOID ROAD RAGE_ Slow Down, Stay Calm, Drive Smart!-thumbnail.png
content.assetUrl: /assets/week 2 media/week 2 new media/AVOID ROAD RAGE_ Slow Down, Stay Calm, Drive Smart!.mp4
content.thumbnailAssetUrl: /assets/week 2 media/week 2 new media/AVOID ROAD RAGE_ Slow Down, Stay Calm, Drive Smart!-thumbnail.png

instructions.caption: You can be right, or you can be alive. Why are we letting strangers on the highway dictate our peace? Drop your ego, save your life. 🙏🏾🚗
instructions.sound: Start with a tense, low-frequency ambient drone (volume 60%) for the first 5 seconds to build tension, cross-fading into a soft, calming gospel piano instrumental (volume 30%) when he mentions praying to God.
instructions.postingTime: Tuesday at 4:30 PM. (Catch people right as they are getting into their cars for the evening rush hour commute. The context is immediate).
instructions.thumbnailStrategy: Freeze on his intense, serious expression around the 0:11 mark. Text overlay in bold, center screen: "I ALMOST KILLED SOMEBODY..."

instructions.caption: How many times has your pride almost cost you everything? 🛑 When someone cuts you off, your flesh wants revenge. But the spiritual sacrifice is letting them "go about their business." Peace is a choice you make at 55 MPH. 👇🏾 Let's talk about it: When was the last time you had to swallow your pride on the road?
instructions.strategyNote: This is about community confession. Pinned comment from the @sacrificial.convo account: "We've all been there. What's your biggest trigger when driving?" Let them vent in the comments to boost the algorithm.
instructions.postingTime: Thursday at 7:00 AM. Give them this perspective right before they hit the morning gridlock.
instructions.thumbnailStrategy: Visual frame of him looking down, shaking his head. Text overlay: "THE COST OF ROAD RAGE 💀"

instructions.caption:
The scary truth about Road Rage... 😳🚗 #Shorts

Description: Is your ego worth your life? In this clip, we discuss the ultimate sacrifice of pride behind the wheel. When we choose peace over retaliation, we let God take the wheel. Catch the full discussion on 'Sacrificial Conversations' every Monday on WFTB 104.1 TabNashville! [Link to full episode]
instructions.pinnedComment: "Either I'm gonna kill somebody, or they're gonna kill me." Have you ever had a moment of clarity like this?
instructions.postingTime: Wednesday at 12:00 PM EST. (Lunch break scrolling).
instructions.thumbnailStrategy: Split screen. Top: The guest speaking passionately. Bottom: Stock footage of blurred, fast-moving highway traffic.
instructions.audienceSettings: Made for kids: NO. Age Restriction: NO.
instructions.relatedVideo: Action: Link this Short directly to the full-length interview. Why: The Short hooks them with the intense "life or death" statement; the full video delivers the complete theological breakdown of surrendering our daily anger.

instructions.caption: We talk a lot about sacrifice on this show—sacrificing our time, our money, our desires. But what about sacrificing our right to be "right"? In this powerful moment from the studio, we tackled something we all deal with: the daily spiritual battle of traffic. When someone cuts you off, disrespects you, or speeds past you, the flesh screams for justice. But as our guest so vulnerably admitted, that pride leads straight to destruction. "Either I'm gonna kill somebody, or they're gonna kill me." God calls us to a radical peace that looks foolish to the world. Letting that aggressive driver pass you isn't weakness; it's the ultimate strength of self-control. Tune in this Monday on WFTB 104.1 TabNashville to hear the rest of this conversation. Watch the full segment here: [Link to YouTube Video]
instructions.shareableQuote: I prayed to God to save me from myself. Now, the speed limit is 70, I go 70. Let them go 90.
instructions.cta: Let's be honest with each other today—how do you maintain your Christian peace when the world is rushing and raging around you? Share your testimony in the comments below!
instructions.postingTime: Sunday at 6:00 PM. People are winding down from the weekend, feeling reflective, and preparing their minds for the Monday morning commute.

Are you holding onto stress that God already told you He would handle? 🛑 Read that again. 

We had to drop this truth bomb from the latest Sacrificial Conversations episode. When you know who gave you your joy, you stop letting temporary problems dictate your permanent peace. 

👇 Are you a spender or a saver when God blesses you? Let's debate in the comments! 😂

instructions.thumbnailStrategy: Frame at 0:41 with him looking directly into the camera. Text overlay: "Why worry about money?"

instructions.caption:
The Secret To Being COMPLETELY Unbothered 🤫🙏 #Shorts

Description: You cannot lose what God has secured. In this clip from Sacrificial Conversations, we dive into what it really means to trust God with your joy and your finances. Stop stressing and start stepping back! Listen to Teddy & Monica live every Monday on WFTB 104.1 TabNashville. Watch the full episode here: [Insert Link]
instructions.pinnedComment: "I asked God to handle it... and then I stepped back." What are you stepping back from today so God can work? Let's talk about it 👇
instructions.postingTime: Thursday at 4:00 PM EST.
instructions.thumbnailStrategy: The wide shot showing everyone listening intently (0:04).
instructions.audienceSettings: Is this video made for kids? Select NO. Age Restriction: Select NO.
instructions.relatedVideo: Action: Link this Short directly to the full-length podcast episode about trusting God with finances. Why: The Short hooks them emotionally with the raw truth; the long-form video provides the deep-dive biblical context they crave.

instructions.caption:
We talk a lot about finding peace, but how many of us are actually willing to surrender control to get it? 

In our latest sit-down, a profound truth was shared: "When I asked God to handle that, I meant it. Then I stepped back." 

It's easy to pray for financial peace or relational joy, but it requires a true sacrifice of our pride to actually take our hands off the steering wheel. If God gave you your joy, no man, no woman, and no bill collector can take it away. 

Watch the full powerful conversation on our YouTube channel [Link] and tune into WFTB 104.1 every Monday!
instructions.shareableQuote: If I know He gave me joy, I don't care what you say. You didn't take my joy at all, because God handled it for me.
instructions.cta: Let's encourage each other today: Drop a testimony in the comments of a time you stepped back and let God handle a stressful financial situation!
instructions.postingTime: Sunday at 3:00 PM EST. (Post-church reflection time when the audience is highly spiritually engaged).

content.title: Unforgiveness Journey_ My 6-Year Path to Freedom
content.assetUrl: /assets/week 2 media/week 2 new media/Unforgiveness Journey_ My 6-Year Path to Freedom.mp4

instructions.caption: 6 years. That's how long it took to finally let go. Unforgiveness is a poison you drink expecting the other person to die. Have you been holding onto something too long? Let's talk about the real cost of holding a grudge. Drop a 🕊️ if you're stepping into renewal this year.
instructions.sound: Ambient, slow cinematic worship instrumental (e.g., "Spontaneous Worship" style). Keep the volume strictly at 10-15% so his deep vocals punch right through the mix.
instructions.postingTime: Tuesday at 8:00 PM EST. (Your audience is winding down, scrolling in bed, and more open to reflective, heavy emotional content).
instructions.thumbnailStrategy: Extreme close-up of his face right at the 0:29 mark. Text overlay in bold, aggressive font: "I held a grudge for 6 YEARS."

instructions.caption: "I went through a 6-year journey of unforgiveness..." 🤯 We talk a lot about grace in church, but we rarely talk about the agonizing, ugly process of actually giving it to someone who hurt us. On this week's Sacrificial Conversations, we talked about the true weight of the name "Forgiven." What are you sacrificing by holding onto that grudge? Your peace? Your joy? 👇 Tell us below: What is the hardest part about forgiving someone who isn't sorry?
instructions.strategyNote: Pin a comment immediately after posting asking, "Is there a difference between forgiving and forgetting?" This will spark an instant theological and emotional debate in the comments, signaling the algorithm to push the Reel wider.
instructions.sound: Trending lo-fi chill beat or a moody instrumental.
instructions.postingTime: Wednesday at 12:00 PM EST. (Catch the Nashville lunch-break crowd when they are looking for a mental reset).
instructions.thumbnailStrategy: Visual frame of him with his hand on his chest (around 0:00). High contrast, warm studio lighting. Text overlay: "The Cost of NOT Forgiving."

instructions.caption:
He Held A Grudge For 6 YEARS... 🤯 #Shorts

Description: Unforgiveness doesn't hurt them, it destroys you. In this raw moment from the Teddy & Monica Show, we dive deep into the exhausting 6-year journey of letting go and finding true renewal. Don't miss the sacrifice. Listen live to the full Sacrificial Conversations broadcast every Monday on WFTB 104.1 TabNashville! Watch the full episode here: [Insert Link]
instructions.pinnedComment: "Unforgiveness is a prison you build for yourself." What's the hardest thing you've ever had to forgive? Let's talk about it.
instructions.postingTime: Thursday at 4:00 PM EST.
instructions.thumbnailStrategy: Frame showing him looking deeply serious/vulnerable.
instructions.audienceSettings: Is this video made for kids? Select NO. Age Restriction: Select NO.
instructions.relatedVideo: Action: Link this Short directly to the full-length YouTube interview. Why: We use the shock value of a 6-year grudge to hook the viewer, leaving them needing to click the full video to find out exactly who he had to forgive and how he finally did it.

instructions.caption: We often toss the word "forgiveness" around like it's cheap and easy. But true forgiveness? It's a sacrifice. It's an agonizing, beautiful, and heavy process of letting go of your right to be angry. In our latest episode of Sacrificial Conversations, our guest opened up about a grueling 6-year journey of dealing with unforgiveness. Six years of carrying weight he wasn't meant to carry. Six years before he fully understood the power of being "Forgiven." It reminds us that renewal isn't always an overnight miracle; sometimes, it's a daily, messy walk. If you're struggling to forgive someone today, know that you aren't alone and the freedom on the other side is worth the fight. Catch the full conversation here: [Insert Link] and join Teddy and Monica this Monday on WFTB 104.1 TabNashville for more!
instructions.shareableQuote: I went through a very, very long journey of dealing with unforgiveness... it took me almost 6 years to be delivered.
instructions.cta: Have you ever had to walk a long road to forgive someone? How did God help you through it? Share your testimony in the comments below—your story might be exactly what someone else needs to read today.
instructions.postingTime: Sunday at 6:00 PM EST. (This is prime time for the faith-based Facebook community as they are reflecting on weekend messages and preparing for the week ahead).

content.title: God is Bigger Than Church: Expanding Your Faith
content.assetUrl: /assets/week 2 media/God is Bigger Than Church_ Expanding Your Faith.mp4

instructions.caption: Are we keeping God in a box? 📦⛪️ Church is A way, but it's not the ONLY way. Stop shrinking God to fit a building.
instructions.sound: Use an ethereal, cinematic ambient pad (like a slowed-down Hans Zimmer track or a moody worship swell). Keep volume at 10-15% to build emotional weight without drowning out his voice.
instructions.postingTime: Sunday at 7:00 PM (Post-church reflection time) or Monday at 8:00 AM (to coincide with the live WFTB broadcast).
instructions.thumbnailStrategy: Select the frame at 0:34 where he puts his hands up emphasizing "God is not that small." Add text overlay: IS CHURCH ENOUGH?

instructions.caption: The hardest truth for some believers to swallow... 🛑👇 Teddy and Monica's guest just said the quiet part out loud: We are making God as small as our church buildings. Yes, the church is a place to gather, learn, and grow. But if your faith stops at the sanctuary doors, you are missing out on the vastness of who God really is. Drop a 🌍 in the comments if you've experienced God outside the four walls of a church!
instructions.strategyNote: The hook here is the debate between traditional religion and personal relationship. Pin a comment that asks: "Has the modern church forgotten how big God is?"
instructions.sound: Trending emotional acoustic guitar or piano instrumental.
instructions.postingTime: 11:30 AM (Catching the lunch break scrollers) or 7:00 PM.
instructions.thumbnailStrategy: Use the frame at 0:13 (Looking direct to camera). Text: CHURCH ISN'T THE ONLY ANSWER.

instructions.caption:
Is Your Church LIMITING God? 🤯⛪️ #Shorts

Description: Why do we try to fit the Creator of the universe into a building? 🌍 On this week's Sacrificial Conversations, we tackle the "evolution of the church." Church is a powerful place to gather and learn, but what happens when we make God only as big as our local congregation? God is not that small! 🎙️ From the Episode: "Expanding Your Faith Beyond the Four Walls" 📻 Listen Live: Mondays on WFTB 104.1 TabNashville 👉 Watch the Full Discussion: Click the "Created from..." link above the title!
instructions.pinnedComment: "Are we missing out on God's blessings because we refuse to look outside the church walls? Let's talk about it. 👇"
instructions.postingTime: 3:00 PM – 5:00 PM EST.
instructions.thumbnailStrategy: Select the frame at 0:00 (High-contrast split screen).
instructions.audienceSettings: Is this video made for kids? Select NO. Age Restriction: Select NO.
instructions.relatedVideo: Action: Link this Short to the full-length interview on your channel. Why: This bold statement hooks people; the full video provides the theological context so they don't just think it's an anti-church rant.

instructions.caption: We love our churches, but have we made God too small? On this week's Sacrificial Conversations on WFTB 104.1, a hard truth was dropped in the studio: 'Church isn't the only answer... unfortunately we make God as big as the church. God is not that small.' It's easy to get comfortable in our usual pews, singing our usual songs, and listening to our usual sermons. Gathering is biblical and beautiful! But when we refuse to embrace the expansive, limit-shattering ways God moves outside the building, we stunt our own spiritual evolution. God is speaking in nature, in our workplaces, in our families, and in the quiet moments of our lives. Let's stop putting a roof over a God who created the stars. Watch the full, eye-opening conversation here. 🎙️ [Link to YouTube]
instructions.shareableQuote: Unfortunately we make God as big as the church. God is not that small.
instructions.cta: Where is a place OUTSIDE of a church building where you have felt God's presence the most? Share your testimony below! 👇
instructions.postingTime: Sunday at 6:00 PM (Evening reflection) or Monday at 9:00 AM.

What My Son Said About Social Media 💔📱 #Shorts

Description: Why is the algorithm pushing destruction? Teddy breaks down a powerful conversation with his son about the sickening glorification of violence online. It's time to push back. 🎙️ From the Episode: "Protecting Peace in a Digital World" 📻 Listen Live: Mondays on WFTB 104.1 TabNashville 👉 Watch the Full Discussion: Click the "Created from..." link above the title!
instructions.pinnedComment: "Are we doing enough to counter the negativity online, or have we just accepted it? Let us know your thoughts below. 👇"

instructions.thumbnailStrategy: Select the frame at 0:08 (Teddy using his hands to emphasize the point, looking directly toward the mic).
instructions.audienceSettings: Is this video made for kids? Select NO. (Though it's about kids, it's targeted at adults/parents). Age Restriction: Select NO.
instructions.relatedVideo: Action: Link this Short to the full-length interview/episode on your channel. Why: This clip hooks the parenting/culture-critique audience; the full video keeps them for the deeper spiritual conversation.

instructions.caption: We had a heartbreaking realization on the show this week... Teddy sat down and shared a conversation he had with his son about the state of social media. When our kids are the ones pointing out how destructive and violent the world has become, we have to stop and listen. Proverbs tells us to guard our hearts, but how do we help our kids guard theirs when their feeds are flooded with negativity? We realized that if we don't actively push out the good—through our music, our videos, and our conversations—the darkness wins by default. We have a responsibility to be the light.
instructions.shareableQuote: If we don't continue to push positive things via music and media, the destructive stuff wins by default.
instructions.cta: Tag a parent, a creator, or a friend who is fighting to put positive content out into the world. Let's encourage them today! 👇

content.title: Music, Ministry & Mental Health: Breaking the Silence
content.assetUrl: /assets/week 2 media/Music, Ministry & Mental Health_ Breaking the Silence.mp4

instructions.caption: Serving on Sunday, suffering on Monday. 🗣️ The Gospel industry has a secret, and the silence is costing lives. It's time to merge ministry and mental health. 🙏🧠
instructions.sound: Use an emotional, cinematic piano sound (e.g., a slowed-down worship instrumental or deep ambient drone like Hans Zimmer's "Time" style). Volume at 15% to let the raw truth hit hard without overpowering the message.
instructions.postingTime: 7:00 PM – 9:00 PM (This is when people are decompressing and scrolling for deep, reflective, authentic content).
instructions.thumbnailStrategy: Use uploaded thumbnail

instructions.caption: Are we praising away the pain, or just hiding it? 🛑👇 The church has been quiet about this for way too long. Our guest drops a massive truth bomb: You can have the Holy Ghost and still need a therapist. You can be bipolar, deal with depression, and still serve. But the pride and the shame? That's what's actually destroying people in the Gospel industry. It's time to make mental health segments as normal as the offering plate. Drop a 🕊️ in the comments if you agree the church needs to talk about this more.
instructions.strategyNote: The hook here is the taboo of being bipolar/depressed while serving. Pin a comment that asks: "Have you ever felt pressured to hide your mental health struggles at church?"
instructions.sound: Trending somber worship audio or minimal instrumental.
instructions.postingTime: 11:00 AM (Pre-lunch break scrolling) or 5:00 PM.
instructions.thumbnailStrategy: Use delivered thumbnail (The one with "MUSIC, MINISTRY & MENTAL HEALTH MERGED").

instructions.caption:
The Dark Secret in the Gospel Industry 🤫⛪ #Shorts

Description: Why are we losing so many talented people in the church? 📉 Because they are too ashamed to ask for help. In this clip, we discuss why the Gospel industry MUST merge music, ministry, and mental health. People are leading worship while battling depression and bipolar disorder—it's time we talk about it. 🎙️ From the Episode: "Breaking the Silence: Mental Health & Ministry" 📻 Listen Live: Mondays on WFTB 104.1 TabNashville 👉 Watch the Full Discussion: Click the "Created from..." link above the title!
instructions.pinnedComment: "Do you think the church does a good job handling mental health, or is there still a massive stigma? Let's talk about it. 👇"

instructions.relatedVideo: Action: Link this Short to the full-length interview on your channel. Why: This clip hooks the church/faith demographic who have felt unseen; the full video gives them the deep spiritual and psychological healing they are looking for.

instructions.caption: Imagine singing songs of deliverance on stage, but feeling completely trapped in your own mind the moment you step off... On this week's Sacrificial Conversations, we tackled one of the most painful, unspoken realities in the church: Mental Health in the Gospel Industry. Our guest said something that should shake every congregation: "We've lost people because of mental health issues because they were prideful, or too ashamed to ask for help." For decades, we've treated mental health struggles as a lack of faith. But the truth is, you can love God, serve faithfully, and still battle depression or bipolar disorder. It's time to stop letting shame steal our brothers and sisters. Let's pray for peace of mind for those serving behind the mic, and let's start making our churches safe spaces for real healing. Watch the full powerful conversation here. 🎙️ [Link to YouTube]
instructions.cta: Have you ever felt like you had to hide your brokenness to be accepted in ministry? Share your thoughts below—your honesty might free someone else today. 👇
instructions.postingTime: 7:30 AM (Morning devotional scrolling) or 8:00 PM (Evening reflection time).

longformSteps.content:
The Hook: What happens when the demand for a performance threatens the purity of your worship? Nate Bean finally tells the truth about surviving Music City.

The Summary: In this raw episode of Sacrificial Conversations, Teddy & Monica sit down with Nashville gospel legend and choir director Nate Bean (founder of 4Given). Broadcasted directly from the WFTB 104.1 TabNashville studios, Nate gets brutally honest about the sacrifices required to maintain the anointing in a city obsessed with the music business.

Timestamps:
* 0:00 Intro: The Reality of Nashville
12:45 The Argument: Ministry vs. Industry
24:30 Why 4Given Sounds Different
38:00 The "Sacrifice": Giving Up the Ego
51:15 Navigating Church Politics
1:05:00 Final Thoughts & A Word of Hope

Links: 📻 Catch the live broadcast every Monday on WFTB 104.1 FM TabNashville! | Follow us: @sacrificial.convo

Share Your Story on the Teddy & Monica Show! 📻
Are you making a positive impact in Nashville? We are currently looking for inspiring guests to join us on the Teddy & Monica Show, broadcasting locally on WFTB 104.1FM and streaming globally on our YouTube podcast, Sacrificial Conversations.
We love diving into meaningful discussions about faith, real life, and the uplifting work happening right here in our community.

Who We’re Looking For:
Community Leaders & Advocates
Artists & Creatives
Entrepreneurs & Business Owners
Anyone with a powerful, faith-driven story to tell!

Why Join Us?
Expand your reach with double the exposure. Your interview will air on traditional FM radio and stream as a video podcast on YouTube, giving your message and your work maximum visibility.

Ready to Book Your Spot?
We would love to hear from you. Reach out to us today to schedule your appearance:
